steps.title: Set up the system of equations
steps.content:
Let $n$ = number of novels and $m$ = number of magazines.

Since there are 11 total items: $$n + m = 11$$
Since the total cost is \$20: $$4n + m = 20$$

steps.title: Solve by elimination
steps.content:
Subtract the first equation from the second to eliminate $m$:
$$(4n + m) - (n + m) = 20 - 11$$
Simplify: $$3n = 9$$
Divide both sides by 3: $$n = 3$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
